questions.q: Magadha was an ancient kingdom centred in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Magadha lay on the Ganges plain in the region of present-day Bihar.

questions.q: From around which century BCE did Magadha rise to dominance?
questions.explanation: Magadha began rising to prominence from around the 6th century BCE.

questions.q: Along which major river system did the Magadhan kingdom develop?
questions.explanation: Magadha was centred on the fertile Ganges (Ganga) plain.

questions.q: What was the earliest capital of Magadha before Pataliputra?
questions.explanation: Rajagriha (Rajgir) was Magadha's early capital before power shifted to Pataliputra.

questions.q: Which later city became the great capital of Magadha?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra, near modern Patna, became Magadha's enduring capital.

questions.q: Magadha is often described as the seedbed of India's first great what?
questions.explanation: Magadha was the cradle from which India's first major empires arose.

questions.q: Which natural resource, abundant near Magadha, gave it a military and economic edge?
questions.explanation: Iron deposits in the region supplied weapons and tools that strengthened Magadha.

questions.q: Magadha's prosperity rested heavily on what quality of its land?
questions.explanation: The fertile Ganges plain made Magadha agriculturally rich and populous.

questions.q: Magadha was one of the great mahajanapadas of which broad period of ancient India?
questions.explanation: Magadha was a leading mahajanapada of the early historic period from the 6th century BCE.

questions.q: Pataliputra, Magadha's later capital, corresponds to which modern city?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra stood at the site of present-day Patna in Bihar.

questions.q: Which dynasty is generally credited with first raising Magadha to dominance in the 6th century BCE?
questions.explanation: The Haryanka dynasty, under Bimbisara and Ajatashatru, made Magadha a leading power.

questions.q: Rajagriha, Magadha's early capital, is identified with which modern town?
questions.explanation: Rajagriha corresponds to modern Rajgir in Bihar.

questions.q: Which neighbouring kingdom was annexed by Magadha to control eastern trade routes?
questions.explanation: Magadha's annexation of Anga gave it access to eastern trade and the Ganges delta.

questions.q: Magadha was a contemporary setting for the lives of which two religious founders?
questions.explanation: Both the Buddha and Mahavira lived and taught in and around Magadha in the 6th-5th centuries BCE.

questions.q: Rajagriha was notably surrounded and protected by what natural feature?
questions.explanation: Rajagriha lay within a ring of hills that gave it strong natural defences.

questions.q: Which dynasty succeeded the Haryankas in ruling Magadha?
questions.explanation: The Shishunaga dynasty followed the Haryankas as rulers of Magadha.

questions.q: Which dynasty, originating in Magadha, founded India's first pan-Indian empire around 321 BCE?
questions.explanation: The Mauryas rose from Magadha to found the first empire spanning most of the subcontinent.

questions.q: Which later imperial dynasty also ruled from Pataliputra, the old Magadhan capital?
questions.explanation: The Gupta Empire of the 4th-6th centuries CE was also centred at Pataliputra.

questions.q: Magadha bordered which republican confederacy across the Ganges to its north?
questions.explanation: The Vajji confederacy, centred on Vaishali, lay north of Magadha across the Ganges.

questions.q: What term is used for the great regional states like Magadha in the 6th century BCE?
questions.explanation: These large states of early historic India are called mahajanapadas.

questions.q: Magadha's military strength was famously aided by its large corps of which animal?
questions.explanation: Magadha's access to war elephants gave its armies an advantage over rivals.

questions.q: Which rival mahajanapada to Magadha's west was eventually absorbed into the growing kingdom?
questions.explanation: Avanti, with its capital at Ujjain, was a major rival before Magadha absorbed it.

questions.q: Which Magadhan king of the Haryanka dynasty is traditionally said to have killed his father Bimbisara to take the throne?
questions.explanation: Buddhist tradition holds that Ajatashatru imprisoned and killed his father Bimbisara.

questions.q: Which Magadhan ruler is traditionally credited with founding the city of Pataliputra by shifting the capital there?
questions.explanation: Udayin, son of Ajatashatru, is credited with making Pataliputra the Magadhan capital.

questions.q: The Nanda dynasty that ruled Magadha before the Mauryas was founded by a king known as what?
questions.explanation: Mahapadma Nanda founded the Nanda dynasty that dominated Magadha in the 4th century BCE.

questions.q: Pataliputra stood near the confluence of the Ganges and which other major river?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra lay near where the Son river met the Ganges, aiding trade and defence.

questions.q: Which Greek ambassador described Magadha's capital Pataliputra in detail during Mauryan times?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes, envoy of Seleucus, described Pataliputra in his work the Indica.

questions.q: Magadha lay within which larger geographic and cultural region of the eastern Ganges plain?
options: Madhyadesha and the eastern Gangetic basin
questions.explanation: Magadha occupied the eastern Gangetic basin, a core part of the northern Indian heartland.

questions.q: The First Buddhist Council, held soon after the Buddha's death, took place at which Magadhan site?
questions.explanation: The First Buddhist Council was convened at Rajagriha, the Magadhan capital.

questions.q: Magadha's rise illustrates a broad shift in early Indian polity from tribal republics toward what?
questions.explanation: Magadha's growth marked the rise of large monarchical states absorbing smaller republics and kingdoms.
